title: ResolvedCeiling
description: Audit-friendly breakdown of the six-axis + risk-classification-table decision produced by evaluate_execution_authority(). Every action dispatch MUST embed this block so a future overlay change never breaks the reproducibility of a past decision. See docs/roadmap/execution-model.md 8. Axes marked with derived_from are computed once inside Axis A and mirrored here for readability (see docs/roadmap/implementation-plan.md, R1).

tier.description: Trust-router tier that produced the finding.

axes.description: One entry per axis. See execution-model.md 2.0-2.6.

winning_axis.description: The axis whose level produced the final min(). The risk_table axis is authoritative when tied.

final_level.description: The min() over all axes. Never higher than any input.

final_quorum.description: Human approvers required. 1 by default; 2 for irreversible per risk-classification.md.

final_path.description: Executor path selected. See execution-model.md 5.4. tool_call is off the substrate-mutation ladder (5.6).

overlay_layers_applied.description: Names of overlay layers that contributed, in application order. See action-ontology.md 7.5.

reason.description: Human-readable justification. English only, no secrets.

derived_from.description: If this axis mirrors a claim already made by Axis A (risk_table), the matched_rule_id from the risk-classification table. See docs/roadmap/implementation-plan.md, R1. Present on static_blast and env when they inherit from Axis A; absent on risk_table itself.

catalog_version.description: risk-classification.yaml version at decision time (Axis A only).

quorum.description: Approver quorum this axis contributes; final_quorum is the max across axes.
